political.s: Cabot's political contributions are bipartisan with minimal PAC donations ($11,689 in 2024). The company maintains moderate lobbying spending ($590,000 in 2024), focusing on regulatory and industry matters affecting specialty chemicals manufacturing.

charity.s: Cabot Corporation Foundation supports over $1 million annually in charitable giving, with focus on STEM education, community development, and health initiatives. The company has a 70+ year tradition of charitable giving through both foundation and site-level programs.
charity.focus: Science and technology education, community engagement, environmental initiatives

environment.s: Cabot achieved platinum EcoVadis rating for fifth consecutive year, placing among top 1% globally. In 2025 CDP assessment, company received A- for Water Security and B for Climate Change, with commitment to net zero emissions by 2050.

labor.s: Cabot achieved industry-leading Total Recordable Incident Rate (TRIR) of 0.20 in 2023, placing in top decile of manufacturing. Company implements comprehensive safety protocols through Responsible Care commitment and has established Drive to Zero injury initiative.

dei.s: Cabot signed CEO Action for Diversity & Inclusion pledge and was named to Newsweek's America's Greatest Workplaces for Diversity 2023, receiving five-star diversity score. Company has established employee resource groups and conducts annual gender pay equity reviews.

privacy.s: Limited publicly available information on data privacy practices. Company handles industrial chemical products and customer data but specific privacy policies not prominently disclosed.

execPay.s: CEO Sean Keohane earned $9.3-9.6 million in total compensation (2024-2025), with significant equity awards. Average employee salary approximately $106k annually, resulting in estimated CEO-to-worker pay ratio of 85:1 to 90:1.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
